summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a stellar ’68 show highlighted by an explosive Alligator, precise St. Stephen and a smoking The Eleven.
review: The Miller/Noel transfer delivers exceptionally clean and balanced audio – the snare on The Eleven is razor sharp and the overall mix is pristine, aside from an isolated one‑second diginoise glitch around Alligator. Musically the performance shines with a peak Alligator jam, exacting St. Stephen and the first‑ever Stephen‑into‑Eleven segue that smokes, making it one of the stronger mid‑year ’68 concerts despite minor set‑list omissions.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews overwhelmingly praise the recording’s clarity and the show’s strong jams, with only isolated complaints about a brief diginoise and slight early‑set balance issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear, clean mix; crisp snare on The Eleven. One‑second diginoise around Alligator (16:41) noted by two reviewers; early set balance a touch off.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens with a three‑song block (TiftOO > Schoolgirl > Alligator) and features a rare 9‑minute Cryptical jam; second set delivers the first known Dark Star → St Stephen → The Eleven sequence, flowing into a solid Death Don't and closing with classic ’68 material.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Les Paul tone is noted as nice and adds character to the performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Snare drum intro on The Eleven highlighted; a middle jam featuring just Garcia and drums, plus a brief Goodnight Jam, are praised.
